title: caucalis infesta subsp. purpurea
intro: Caucalis infesta subsp. purpurea, also known as the purple devil's-bit, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Caucalis infesta subsp. purpurea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
